title: Iridonian (species)
content:
{{Top|fga|leg}} {{Youmay|the Iridonians, a warrior people|[[Zabrak/Legends|Zabraks]]}} {{Species |image=[[File:Iridonian.jpg]] |name=Iridonian |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor= |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=*Bipedal *Violent |lifespan= |origin= |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|Honor comes from slaying your opponent, and the true reward of any job is the taste of your foe's dying blood on your tongue.|An Iridonian mercenary|}} The '''Iridonians,''' colloquially called Iridorians, were a warrior people with a particularly [[Blood/Legends|bloodthirsty]] reputation. Most [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] citizens feared them, for it was rumored that they would continue to attack a corpse even after the target was [[Death/Legends|dead]] and turn against each other in battle when their bloodlust overwhelmed them. The Iridonians were rivals to the [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] and [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] cultures, and members of the [[Species/Legends|species]] were found on such [[Planet/Legends|planets]] as [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]], a period of galactic unrest between [[3959 BBY|3959]] and [[3956 BBY]]. The amnesiac [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] encountered an [[Unidentified Iridonian mercenary|Iridonian mercenary]] on Manaan during the war. The [[Armor/Legends|armored]] warrior told Revan of his people's history before Revan continued on his quest for the [[Star Map|maps]] that would lead him to the [[Star Forge]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] of the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|Are you deaf? I asked for Iridonian hearts! Not Rodian, not Ithorian! Iridonian!''"
"''What do you want me to do, put 'em back?''"
"''Try stealing a brain. You could use one.|Two organ harvesters on Nar Shaddaa|audio=OrganHarvesters-TOR.flac|{{TORcite|Nar Shaddaa}}}} The Iridonians were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with opposable thumbs and [[Heart/Legends|hearts]]. An [[Unidentified Iridonian mercenary|Iridonian mercenary]] found on [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]] wore full-body [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and was approximately the same height as the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]. ==Society and culture== {{Quote|Those savages keep hacking at a fallen foe even after death, mutilating the corpse out of pure blood lust.|[[Ergeron]]|}} Iridonian society was based on a lust for [[Blood/Legends|bloodshed]] and violence.''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' They held a traditional rivalry with the [[near-Human]] [[Echani/Legends|Echani]] [[Species/Legends|species]] and with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior culture. The Iridonians dismissed the code of honor Echani held dear, while they despised the Mandalorians for prioritizing [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] over bloodshed. Iridonian society's bloodlust was reflected by the words of a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] of the species, who told the amnesiac [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Revan that Iridonians did not have friends. That was evident in the eyes of non-Iridonians; one man reported that in the absence of a foe, they would turn on each other for the sheer thrill of bloodshed. They were believed to continue attacking opponents' bodies even after a victim's [[Death/Legends|death]]. The Iridonians manufactured specialized weapons and armor for their own use, although for much of galactic history, those designs were officially outlawed. Such items commanded a premium, costing 1,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] more than a typical non-Iridonian example of that item, or 10% more for larger goods. ==History== {{Quote|I even heard they'll turn on each other when battle fever hits. Who wants to work with someone like that?|Ergeron|}} Iridonians were a rare sight in the civilized [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], even during the [[Jedi Civil War]], a conflict between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] beginning in [[3959 BBY]]. By [[3956 BBY]], both sides in the conflict sought to recruit Iridonians to fight for their respective sides due to their reputation as powerful, if eccentric, warriors—a product of their cultural ethos of savagery. Revan encountered one such Iridonian mercenary on the planet Manaan. That gun-for-hire had been recruited by the Sith to gather youth of the [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] species, native to the water planet, to the [[Sith Embassy]] for training in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Because Iridonian weapons and armor were illegal in much of the galaxy, Iridonians faced discrimination when dealing with members of law enforcement agencies and other government organizations. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]], Iridonian hearts were traded on the black market by organ harvesters. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|Oh…one ''[error]'' I noticed really early, but kept forgetting to point out…the Iridonian Manufacture gear template should be Iridorian Manufacture. With an R, not an N.''"
"''Actually, it shouldn't. We were specifically instructed by LFL to call it Iridonian and that it was the KOTOR video game that was in error.|[[Rodney Thompson]] responds to a post on the Iridonians|{{WebCite|url=http://forums.gleemax.com/showpost.php?p=16881789&postcount=111|text=Errors of the Old Republic Campaign Guide|work=the [[Wizards of the Coast]] message boards|author=[[Rodney Thompson]]|date=2008-09-20|nobackup=1}}}} An "Iridorian" appears in the ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]] produced by [[BioWare]] in [[2003]]. The individual appears on the planet Manaan wearing full body armor, so his actual appearance remains a mystery. However, he does inform the character Revan, controlled by the player, of the history of his species. Later, in [[2008]], the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' was released by [[Wizards of the Coast]]. It lists a gear template for "Iridonian Manufacture," which describes Iridonians as "''a mysterious species known for their savagery and aggressiveness''." The gear template gives Iridonian weapons and armor extra resistance to bludgeoning attacks and a bonus to certain attacks that emphasize brute strength. This description matches that of the Manaan Iridorian, but not the pre-existing species known alternatively as both Iridonians and [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabraks]], who are said to be a more peaceful species. This change, from "Iridorian" to "Iridonian," was confirmed to be deliberate by [[Rodney Thompson]], one of authors of the ''Campaign Guide'', who explained that [[Lucasfilm]] stipulated the change and that it was "the KOTOR video game that was in error." [[Bao-Dur]] is referred to as an Iridonian in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' by [[G0-T0]]. ==Appearances== *[[Knights of the Old Republic 37|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 37]] {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{C|First appearance, as Iridorian}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Sentient species]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Iridonians]] }}
